subject: Sociology
title: Social Mobility
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Social Mobility.

hook.text: Your grandfather was a farmer, your father a teacher, and you're a lawyer. That upward climb is social mobility.

buildup.text: Social mobility is movement between social positions, either within a lifetime or across generations.

discovery.text: Intergenerational mobility compares parents to children. Intragenerational tracks one person's career trajectory.

twist.text: America calls itself the land of opportunity, but mobility rates are lower there than in Denmark or Canada.

climax.text: Structural factors — education access, healthcare, housing — determine mobility far more than individual ambition.

punchline.text: Mobility depends on whether the ladder reaches your floor.

quiz.question: What is intergenerational mobility?
options: Movement in social position between parents and children
Job changes within a single career
